rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant NMBFil, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant BASF Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs submitted evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Rubino was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Bondo auto body filler) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue two (third cause of action) and denied as to issues one and three (sophisticated user doctrine and claim for punitive damages). As to the sophisticated user doctrine, plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether the sophisticated user doctrine does not apply. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to demonstrate that Michigan law applies to that claim and failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to adjudication of the third cause of action, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant required the use of asbestos-containing friction components. Plaintiffs objections to the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply are sustained. The court did not consider that evidence. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
